title: First Solar's Reliance On 45X Tax Credit Triggers Risks As Backlog Tapers
text: First Solar's strong Q4 2025 and Q1 2026 earnings are heavily dependent on Section 45X tax credit monetization, which contributes approximately $2.1-2.2 billion to 2026 earnings through gross margin expansion. This structural support creates both opportunities and risks for investors. The positive aspect is that the company has secured substantial tax credit monetization agreements through sales arrangements with financial institutions, providing earnings visibility. However, longer-term risks include Section 45X phase-out scheduled for 2033 and potential policy changes under new administrations. The company's 47.9 GW contracted backlog provides multi-year revenue visibility, but the quality of these contracts may be questioned if they depend on tax credit pass-through arrangements. As the backlog tapers post-2028, the company's earnings power will depend increasingly on CURE technology adoption and margin improvement. Additionally, tariff headwinds of $125-135 million net in 2026 partially offset tax credit benefits. Investors should monitor backlog additions in 2026 and the company's ability to secure long-term contracts without heavy dependence on government incentives. The perovskite technology development scheduled for 2027-2028 represents a potential game-changer but carries execution risk. Overall, First Solar's current earnings are supported by cyclical factors (tax credits, tariff benefits) rather than pure business fundamentals, warranting careful valuation analysis.
